channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Hierarchical heterogeneous ordering enables wide-temperature strength-ductility synergy in additively manufactured medium-entropy alloys
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72261-7

An additively manufactured medium entropy alloy with hierarchical ordering maintains high strength and ductility from 77 to 873 K, offering a pathway to structural materials capable of reliable performance across wide temperature ranges.
item.dc:title: Hierarchical heterogeneous ordering enables wide-temperature strength-ductility synergy in additively manufactured medium-entropy alloys
item.dc:creator: Yun Cheng
Bo Li
Fu-Zhen Xuan

item.title: Author Correction: Mapping in situ the assembly and dynamics in aqueous supramolecular polymers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72528-z

Author Correction: Mapping in situ the assembly and dynamics in aqueous supramolecular polymers
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Mapping in situ the assembly and dynamics in aqueous supramolecular polymers
item.dc:creator: Huachuan Du
Ruomeng Qiu
Xianwen Lou
Stef A. H. Jansen
Hiroaki Sai
Yuyang Wang
Albert J. Markvoort
E. W. Meijer
Samuel I. Stupp

item.title: High-strength and high-modulus silicon monoxide for high-energy-density and fast-charging lithium-ion batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72434-4

Energy-dense silicon-based negative electrodes repeatedly expand and shrink during battery operation, causing mechanical damage that degrades performance. Here, authors develop a deformation-resistant silicon-based negative electrode, enabling durable fast charging and high-energy operation.
item.dc:title: High-strength and high-modulus silicon monoxide for high-energy-density and fast-charging lithium-ion batteries
item.dc:creator: Minjun Je
Yeong Hun Choi
Seung Hyun Jeong

Soojin Park

Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72307-w

Author Correction: Blue organic light-emitting diode with a turn-on voltage of 1.47V
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Blue organic light-emitting diode with a turn-on voltage of 1.47V
